items.text:
NOTICE: Temporary Water Shutoff in Brunner Hall

The Facilities Office will shut off water service in Brunner Hall so that aging pipes can be replaced. Residents are asked to prepare for the interruption in advance.

- Date: Tuesday, April 14
- Time: 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM
- Affected area: all residential floors

During these hours, restrooms and kitchens on every floor will be closed. Bottled water will be provided in the lobby at no charge. A backup shower station has been set up in the Recreation Center; a $5 refundable key deposit is required for access.

Residents who need special accommodations must contact the Housing Desk before April 12. Questions may be directed to facilities@campus.edu.

format_metadata.subject: Temporary Water Shutoff in Brunner Hall

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will be provided to residents at no charge?

options.A: A refundable key deposit at the front desk
options.B: A backup shower in every residence hall
options.C: A supply of bottled water in the lobby
options.D: A full refund for the affected room fees

questions.explanation: The notice states that 'Bottled water will be provided in the lobby at no charge,' so the free item is the bottled water in the lobby.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To inform residents about a temporary water shutoff
options.B: To announce the closing of the Recreation Center
options.C: To request donations toward new building pipes
options.D: To explain how to reserve a campus shower room

questions.explanation: The notice's central aim is to tell residents that water service will be shut off temporarily and how to prepare; the shower station and deposit are supporting details, not the main point.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about residents who want to shower during the shutoff?

options.A: They will be charged a nightly usage fee
options.B: They must move to another residence hall
options.C: They will need to leave a deposit for a key
options.D: They cannot wash until the water returns

questions.explanation: The notice says the backup shower station requires 'a $5 refundable key deposit' for access, so a resident who wants to shower must leave a deposit to get a key.
